summary: The implementation satisfies all contract requirements. ProgressLoader is correctly implemented with spinner frames, setMessage, handleInput (Esc/Ctrl+C), stop, and render methods. runPrWorkflow is extracted with progress.setMessage calls at all 5 phase transitions. handleWorktreePr wraps the workflow with ctx.ui.custom when UI is available, and falls back to direct invocation otherwise. Existing info() calls are preserved. No external dependencies are added.

findings.title: Cancellation via onAbort passes Error through done() as WorktreeMetadata
findings.details: When Esc/Ctrl+C is pressed, onAbort calls done(new Error('Cancelled') as unknown as WorktreeMetadata). The .then() handler after ctx.ui.custom checks `result instanceof Error` and re-throws. However, if runPrWorkflow is mid-flight and also rejects (e.g., the abort signal isn't propagated to operations), done() could be called twice — once from onAbort and once from the .catch. This is a minor robustness concern but doesn't violate the contract since the contract only requires 'throw an error to halt the PR operation'.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a guard flag to ensure done() is only called once, preventing potential double-resolution.

strengths: ProgressLoader is cleanly implemented as a self-contained class with all required methods (setMessage, handleInput, stop, render) matching the contract specifications exactly.
All 10 spinner frames (⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏) are present with the correct 80ms interval.
The extraction of runPrWorkflow preserves all existing info() calls while adding progress.setMessage calls at all 5 specified phase transitions with the correct message strings.
The handleWorktreePr wrapper correctly branches on ctx.hasUI && ctx.ui?.custom, maintaining backward compatibility for non-UI consumers.
No external dependencies are introduced — ProgressLoader uses only the MinimalTui type derived from the existing tui interface.
The stop() method is called on both success (.then) and error (.catch) paths, preventing interval leaks.
